title: rinorea obtusa
intro: Rinorea obtusa, also known as the small-leaved viola or Malayan viola, is a flowering plant species in the family Violaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Rinorea obtusa is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and relieving respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, and is often used in traditional medicine practices in various parts of Asia.
